observations.title: Never use dangerouslySetInnerHTML with unsanitized data
facts: XSS vector — attacker-controlled HTML executes in user's browser
If needed, sanitize with DOMPurify first
Block: dangerouslySetInnerHTML with user input or API data

observations.title: Include all dependencies in useEffect dependency arrays
facts: Missing deps cause stale closure bugs
Use eslint exhaustive-deps rule
Use functional updates to avoid dependency on state

observations.title: Use stable keys for list items — never use array index for dynamic lists
facts: Index keys cause bugs with reordering, deletion, insertion
Use database IDs, UUIDs, or other stable identifiers
Index keys are only safe for static, non-reorderable lists

observations.title: Return cleanup functions from useEffect for subscriptions and timers
facts: Prevent memory leaks from setInterval, addEventListener, WebSocket
Cleanup runs on unmount and before effect re-runs
Use AbortController for fetch requests

observations.title: Never call hooks conditionally — always call in the same order
facts: Hooks rely on call order for identity
Don't put hooks inside if/else, loops, or after early returns
Move conditions inside the hook instead

observations.title: Don't mutate state directly — always create new references
facts: React detects changes by reference comparison
Use spread: {...state, key: val} for objects
Use map/filter/spread for arrays, never push/splice

observations.title: Use Error Boundaries at route level and around risky components
facts: Unhandled render errors crash the entire app
Error boundaries catch errors in render, lifecycle, constructors
Don't catch event handler errors — use try/catch there

observations.title: Virtualize long lists — don't render 1000+ DOM nodes
facts: Use react-window or react-virtuoso
Only render visible rows
Measure first — lists under 100 items rarely need this

observations.title: Handle loading, error, and empty states explicitly
facts: Every data-fetching component needs all three states
Use Suspense + ErrorBoundary for the pattern
Show skeletons over spinners for better UX

observations.title: Use React.memo only after measuring — premature memoization adds overhead
facts: React.memo has a cost (shallow comparison per render)
Only useful when component re-renders often with same props
Pair with useCallback/useMemo for prop stability
